figures.caption: Table 3. Test njNLL for the synthetic four channel bifurcation task.
Figure 4. Comparing samples from ProFITi and CircuITS for the four channel bifurcation task.

Figure 1. Demonstrating MOSES inability to learn a multivariate bifurcation with 4 independent channels. MOSES-K, refers to MOSES with K-many mixture components. The plots of CircuITS are created by using 2 circuit components.
Table 7. Comparing the MSE of the mean estimated by 1000 samples from probabilistic models on the 36-3⋆ setting.

Table 6. Comparison of njNLL and mNLL on the 36-3⋆ task. Lower values are better. Best results are in bold, second best in italics. Results are taken from (Yalavarthi et al., 2025b). OOM stands for out-of-memory.
Figure 3. Detailed architecture of the CircuITS Encoder with tensor dimensions. The global context H̃ (dim C ×D) branches to generate leaf contexts (dim D) and circuit weights.

Table 1. Comparison of njNLL and mNLL for different forecasting splits. Lower values are better. Best results are in bold, second best in italics. The ⋆ represents that the forecasting horizon includes the next n steps, instead of n-many hours/months. For all tasks on the Physionet’12 dataset and the complete 36–3⋆ task, we report results from Yalavarthi et al. (2025b).
Table 9. Energy Score and CRPS of competing models. Lower values are better. Best results are in bold, second best in italics. Models are trained and tuned on njNLL.
Figure 5. Sensitivity Analysis on the 36-12 task for the number of circuit components K.
Figure 6. Comparing memory consumption and epoch times during training on Toy datasets.
